summary: Introduces equine agility as a growing sport, covering its accessibility to all horses and the fun, obstacle-based nature of in-hand and mounted agility competitions.
Cavallo's president recounts judging the Certified Professional of the Year award for PATH International, highlighting therapeutic horsemanship instructors and equine-assisted therapy.
First-person reflection on a confrontation with farriers while speaking on hoof care, advocating barefoot-and-booted horses and respectful dialogue over polarized camps.
Testimonial-driven post from Hawaii about using Cavallo hoof boots in parades instead of shoes, citing heat conduction concerns and a University of Zurich shod-horse study.
Recap of Carole's well-attended talk at Equine Affaire in Massachusetts on barefoot horse care, noting strong turnout from owners, vets, doctors, and farriers.
Trail-riding safety tips for hunting season, covering where hunting is permitted, tracking local seasons, wearing blaze orange, and attaching bells to tack.
Cold-weather pet safety guide covering winter wellness exams, cold tolerance limits, and adjusting care for arthritic, short-haired, and diabetic pets.
New Year reflection on how small daily choices compound into long-term health and wealth, tying hoof trimming and Cavallo boots into preventative horse care.
Four tips for preparing for your first riding clinic, including researching the clinician and staying vigilant about what training methods you accept for your horse.
Profile of liberty horse trainer Emma Massingale and her 61-mile Scottish Highlands trek with two young ponies, sponsored by Cavallo Trek Boots.
First-person account of caring for horses in harsh winter conditions, covering frozen buckets and bedding and the resolve required to keep animals fed and watered.
Announces the Cavallo Trek as the #1 hoof boot in the world per EZVid Wiki's 2017 Best Horse Boots, detailing its ProMesh design and uses for hoof conditions and rehabilitation.
